topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box smelled faintly of old paper and lavender. I stared at the contents—a meticulous, color-coded collection of every worksheet, every essay, every completed project from my entire schooling. My hands trembled as I lifted a crumpled history assignment from the top. It was a perfect, pristine copy. A hollow ache settled in my chest, heavy and suffocating. I sank onto the floor, the sheer volume of completed work pressing down on me. "Mom?" I whispered, the sound catching in my throat. The silence in the room seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

I found the hidden bin tucked behind a loose floorboard in the attic. Inside, stacked like tiny, forgotten monuments, were the remnants of my academic life. Seeing the sheer dedication poured into these papers made my stomach clench. I ran a hand over a stack of math proofs, my knuckles white. A tightness gripped my jaw. I didn't move to touch them, just stood there, breathing shallowly, the air feeling too thin in my lungs. A strange, hot prickle started behind my eyes, and I quickly blinked hard, trying to force the feeling away.

[story 3]

It was a shocking discovery. Tucked inside an old shoebox, I found the complete portfolio of my middle school science projects. The weight of expectation, suddenly tangible, made my shoulders droop. I picked up a detailed diagram, tracing the lines with a fingertip, a strange wave of disorientation washing over me. My gaze drifted to the window, where the afternoon sun seemed too bright, too harsh. I let out a slow, shaky exhale, the sound barely audible, as if releasing a pressure I hadn't realized I was holding.
